text.title: Creating or Updating Claude Code Agents Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the agents methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Use Agent when context must be isolated AND parallel execution is needed. Use Skill when context can be shared AND the work is knowledge-bound. Use Command when invocation is manual AND scope is < 250 lines.

rationale.source: Claude Code Sub-agents docs; Skills docs; Commands docs

rule.rationale: Three components solve different problems; mixing them up wastes context (agents) or removes auto-trigger (skills) or over-engineers a simple action (commands).

rule.statement: Agent definition MUST declare the minimum tool whitelist needed (typically ≤ 5 tools). Defaulting to all tools is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Over-broad tool surface increases blast radius on a misbehaving agent; tight whitelist is the primary safety mechanism.

rule.statement: Parent-agent IPC MUST be via files (paths written by parent + read by agent + result file written by agent). Shared in-memory state is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Agents have isolated processes; in-memory state crosses neither process nor context boundary.

rule.statement: Every agent MUST declare an output contract (JSON shape or Markdown sections) that the parent parses programmatically.
rule.rationale: Without contract, parent has to LLM-parse the agent's free text, defeating the parallelism win.

rule.statement: Agent prompt MUST be self-contained: all inputs provided upfront. Mid-run input requests are forbidden (agent has no user channel).
rule.rationale: Task tool runs agents non-interactively; mid-run input requests deadlock the agent.
